title: Repo + pnpm/TS/ESM + CI (build/test/lint) + static-site generator decision
context: Patient-Advocate-Research-Primer needs a working repository skeleton before any content or tooling tasks can proceed. The project uses TypeScript + ESM + pnpm workspaces (matching Elyos conventions), and needs a CI pipeline (build/test/lint) plus a static-site generator to serve educational content accessibly. The static-site generator choice (Astro vs Eleventy vs plain) is an open decision to be made and recorded here.
objective: Stand up the repo skeleton with pnpm/TS/ESM workspace configuration, a passing CI pipeline (build/test/lint), and a decided and configured static-site generator — giving all subsequent tasks a clean, green base to build on.
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm workspaces configured with at least packages/build and site packages; tsconfig with ESM output; lint (ESLint or oxlint) and format (Prettier) configured.
CI pipeline (GitHub Actions) passes: pnpm build, pnpm test, pnpm lint all green on the skeleton.
Static-site generator chosen (Astro, Eleventy, or plain) and documented in an ADR committed to the repo; reason recorded (accessibility, offline-capability, printability, pnpm/TS fit).
Chosen static-site generator installed, hello-world page renders, and a npm/pnpm build script produces the static output.
README and CONTRIBUTING stubs committed; output/ and review/ directories present with .gitkeep.
output: A merged PR containing the repo skeleton: pnpm/TS/ESM workspace config, CI pipeline, static-site generator setup with hello-world, and ADR documenting the generator choice.
